summary.intent: Fix a false 'update available' banner showing in local dev builds, and confirm that the upcoming YYYYMMDD.XX versioning scheme will correctly trigger updates for existing users on 0.10.18.
summary.outcome: Added a `is_dev_build()` guard in version.rs and update_check.rs to skip update checks when MOLTIS_VERSION env var is not set. Confirmed date-based versions are already handled correctly for update triggering. Changes committed and pushed to main.

repo: Dev builds without MOLTIS_VERSION env var default to Cargo.toml version 0.1.0, which is less than any published semver release, causing false update banners.

The update check compares local version against releases.json; the is_newer_version() function at line 119 of update_check.rs already treats any date-based version (YYYYMMDD.XX) as newer than any semver version.
The release pipeline's update-deploy-tags job updates releases.json only for non-dry-run, non-pre-release tagged releases.

code.finding: Added is_dev_build() const that returns true when MOLTIS_VERSION is not set at compile time.
is_newer_version() treats date-based versions as automatically newer than semver versions, so YYYYMMDD.XX will correctly notify 0.10.18 users.
Updated fetch_update_availability to early-return when is_dev_build() is true.

workflow: Push required a YubiKey tap for SSH signing, and the remote had diverged requiring a rebase before the push succeeded.
Running the full update_check test suite (16 tests) was sufficient to validate the change before committing.

friction: Push blocked on YubiKey tap requirement, requiring a manual retry.
Remote had new commits requiring a rebase before push could succeed.
